question: Illustrate access specifiers (`public`, `private`, `protected`). Create a base class `Person` with `public` name, `protected` age, and `private` socialSecurityNumber. Derive a `Student` class and try to access these members, explaining which are accessible and why.

test_cases.expected:
--- Access from main function ---
Accessing public member 'name': Alice Smith
Attempting to access protected member 'age': Not accessible directly from main (outside derived class).
Attempting to access private member 'socialSecurityNumber': Not accessible directly from main.

--- Student Info (from Student class) ---
Accessing public member 'name': Alice Smith
Accessing protected member 'age': 20
Student ID: S1001
Attempting to access private member 'socialSecurityNumber': Not accessible directly from Student class.

driver: int main() { Student s("Alice Smith", 20, "123-45-6789", "S1001"); std::cout << "--- Access from main function ---" << std::endl; std::cout << "Accessing public member 'name': " << s.name << std::endl; std::cout << "Attempting to access protected member 'age': Not accessible directly from main (outside derived class)." << std::endl; std::cout << "Attempting to access private member 'socialSecurityNumber': Not accessible directly from main." << std::endl; s.displayStudentInfo(); return 0; }
errors: Missing semicolon after class definition 'Person'. C++ requires a semicolon after class, struct, and union definitions.
